finding: After ATM-GOV-0173 closed successfully, runner-sync left .atm/history/evidence/ATM-GOV-0173.runner-sync-receipt.json and .atm/history/session-events/<laneId>/... untracked. The governed git wrapper could not commit the runner-sync receipt after close because pre-commit classified the singleton evidence file as ATM_PROTECTED_STATE_EVIDENCE_FILE_MISSING_TASK_CONTEXT, while residue classified both the receipt and lane session event as manual-review with no requiredCommand.
expectedBehavior: Runner-sync release should either stage/commit its receipt and broker lane events in the close bundle, write them to a committable record-only surface, or classify them as safe runtime residue with an exact cleanup command. A completed task should not leave the operator choosing between dirty worktree residue and unguided manual deletion.
evidenceOrRepro: ATM-GOV-0173 on 2026-07-18: runner-sync enqueue/release for stewardWorkId runner-sync-dad1be23 succeeded, taskflow close --write auto-committed target close 09aa7ec6600a58c1eb940408032275ec1b781832 and planning close 1b1ea179ff9121446c6adf30881b4fff8bce510b, then node atm.mjs git commit --task ATM-GOV-0173 --auto-stage failed on .atm/history/evidence/ATM-GOV-0173.runner-sync-receipt.json with ATM_PROTECTED_STATE_EVIDENCE_FILE_MISSING_TASK_CONTEXT. node atm.mjs residue --json reported the receipt and lane event as manual-review.
followUp: Add a formal runner-sync receipt/lane-event persistence path: include those artifacts in taskflow close commit bundles when runnerGateDecision is required, or add a governed runner-sync record-commit/cleanup verb and residue classifier coverage with regression tests.
